title: thymus serpyllum var. kosteleckyanus
intro: Thymus serpyllum var. kosteleckyanus, also known as creeping thyme or wild thyme, is a low-growing, spreading herb native to Europe and North Africa, characterized by its tiny leaves and small pink or white flowers. The medicinal properties of Thymus serpyllum var. kosteleckyanus include its antimicrobial, antifungal, and antiseptic properties, which make it effective in treating wounds, cuts, and skin infections. The health benefits of using this plant include reducing stress, improving respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and coughs, and aiding in digestion. Thymus serpyllum var. kosteleckyanus can be prepared in various herbal forms, including teas, infusions, tinctures, and essential oils, which can be used to promote overall well-being and support immune function.
